description_long: This is a vibrant red flower with large, broad petals that radiate outward from a central point, creating a striking and bold appearance. The petals are slightly ruffled at the edges and display subtle gradients of red, with some areas appearing deeper and others lighter. At the center, several white-tipped stamens and a prominent pistil extend outward, adding contrast and detail to the flower's core. The flower is attached to a thick, greenish stem that shows some branching and a few remnants of dried floral parts. The overall structure is robust, with the petals overlapping and forming a somewhat funnel-shaped bloom. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as decorative plantings along sidewalks.
description: A large, vivid red flower with broad, overlapping petals and prominent white-tipped stamens, attached to a thick green stem.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the open, symmetrical face of the flower, showcasing the full spread of its red petals and central stamens.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the trumpet-like shape of the flower, with the petals curving outward and the stem and floral tube clearly visible.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the base of the petals and the thick, branching stem, with some petal overlap and subtle color variation.
description_view_3: Another side view, showing the elongated profile of the flower and the way the petals flare outward from the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, overlapping at the base and spreading outward symmetrically from the center, forming a whorled pattern typical of many ornamental flowers.
